texto: I don’t know if it was just me making things up in my head but after the fear in their eyes had gone what replaced it was like a sad kind of wondering. A wondering of where the old me was hiding. A wondering about where the old me had gone to. It was like I had suddenly been taken over by someone else and they could see the old me had fallen away for good.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kerem Mermutlu: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de enfrentar incertezas com ação. O trecho “I don’t know if it was just me making things up in my head but after the fear in their eyes had g…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que coragem, coming-of-age-stories, teen pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de teen e young-adult-fiction.

texto: I work in a restaurant in an airport in Taiwan. I am eighteen years old and I don’t like my job because everyone gets on planes and leaves. And I want to leave too.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kerem Mermutlu: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a trabalho. O trecho “I work in a restaurant in an airport in Taiwan. frase_original: I work in a restaurant in an airport in Taiwan. I am eighteen years old and I don’t like my job because everyone gets on planes and leaves. And I want to leave too.

texto: Something else you should really know about me. When I get nervous, my fingers shake. I’ve noticed this a lot recently. When mother and father argue and their voices are falling around the small family apartment, when their voices are banging against my bedroom door, I can feel my fingers start to move. I tell my fingers to stop and, sometimes, they do. But if I look at my hands closely, once I’ve told them to stop, and I try to focus on keeping them as still as possible, I notice that they are still moving.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Kerem Mermutlu sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “Something else you should really know about me. When I get nervous, my fingers shake. I’ve notice…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, teen, young-adult-fiction, útil em momentos de young-adult-fiction.

frase_original: Something else you should really know about me. When I get nervous, my fingers shake. I’ve noticed this a lot recently. When mother and father argue and their voices are falling around the small family apartment, when their voices are banging against my bedroom door, I can feel my fingers start to move. I tell my fingers to stop and, sometimes, they do. But if I look at my hands closely, once I’ve told them to stop, and I try to focus on keeping them as still as possible, I notice that they are still moving.
